content:
A Simple Question
Imagine you ask an AI assistant:
“Create a Kubernetes Deployment with three replicas.”
You press Enter.
Within a few seconds, the AI generates a complete deployment file.
It almost feels like the AI read your sentence exactly the way another person would.
But that’s not what actually happens.
Computers don’t understand English, Telugu, Hindi, or any other human language.
They only understand numbers.
So before an AI model can process your request, it performs two important steps:

• It breaks your sentence into smaller pieces called Tokens.

• It converts those tokens into numbers called Embeddings.
These two concepts are the foundation of every modern Generative AI system.

What Are Tokens?
Think about reading a book.
You don’t read the entire page at once.
You naturally read it word by word.
AI follows a similar idea.
Instead of processing a complete sentence in one step, it first breaks the sentence into smaller pieces called Tokens.
A token is simply a small unit of text.
Depending on the AI model, a token can be:

• A complete word

• Part of a word

• A punctuation mark

• A number

• Or even a single character
For example, consider this sentence:
“AI is changing the world.”
A model might break it into tokens like this:

• AI

• is

• changing

• the

• world

• .
Instead of seeing one long sentence, the AI now sees a sequence of smaller pieces that are easier to process.
Key Takeaway: Tokens are the basic building blocks that AI uses to process text.
Why Doesn’t AI Read Whole Sentences?
Imagine someone gives you a 500-page book and asks you to understand everything in one glance.
That’s impossible.
Humans naturally process information in smaller pieces.
AI does the same.
Breaking text into tokens makes it easier to analyze relationships between words, understand context, and generate accurate responses.

What Are Embeddings?
Now comes the most interesting part.
Suppose the model has already converted your sentence into tokens.
Can it understand those tokens directly?
No.
Remember…
Computers only understand numbers.
So every token must now be converted into a numerical representation.
This numerical representation is called an Embedding.
An embedding is a list of numbers that represents the meaning of a token.
You don’t need to understand the mathematics behind it.
Just remember this simple idea:
Words become numbers before AI can understand them.

Why Are Embeddings So Powerful?
Consider these words:

• King

• Queen

• Prince

• Princess
To us, these words are related.
Interestingly, embeddings capture these relationships using numbers.
Similarly:

• Dog and Puppy are closely related.

• Apple and Banana are more closely related than Apple and Car.

• Kubernetes and Docker are closer than Kubernetes and Bicycle.
This means embeddings don’t just represent words.
They also capture their meaning and relationships.
That’s one of the reasons modern AI systems generate such meaningful responses.
Key Takeaway: Embeddings help AI understand the meaning and relationship between words — not just the words themselves.
Bringing Everything Together
Let’s see the complete journey.
You type:
“Explain Kubernetes.”
The AI performs these steps:
Step 1: Receives your sentence.
↓
Step 2: Breaks it into Tokens.
↓
Step 3: Converts every Token into an Embedding.
↓
Step 4: Processes those Embeddings using its Neural Network.
↓
Step 5: Generates a response.
This entire process happens in a fraction of a second.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

From the user’s perspective, it feels like the AI instantly understood the question.
But behind the scenes, a lot is happening.

Final Thoughts
When we interact with AI, it feels like we’re having a conversation with another person.
In reality, AI never reads our sentences the way humans do.
It first breaks them into tokens.
Then it converts those tokens into embeddings.
Only after that can the model understand the relationships between words and generate meaningful responses.
These concepts may seem simple, but they are the foundation of every modern Large Language Model.
In the next article, we’ll explore one of the biggest breakthroughs in Artificial Intelligence:
What is the technology behind today’s Generative AI, and how can we interact with it effectively?
To answer that, we’ll learn about the LLM and Prompt Engineering .
Thank you for reading!
If you’re enjoying this series, stay tuned for Part 4 of “Generative AI, One Concept at a Time.”
